page: Amazon Web Services r6gd.large is a memory-optimized instance powered by a 2.5 GHz AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor. This server features an arm64 architecture with 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs, operating without hyperthreading. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Local storage is provided via a bundled 118 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, and baseline network bandwidth is rated at 1 Gbps. The instance runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark results indicate that the r6gd.large delivers moderate single-core performance but exhibits limitations under multi-core and memory-intensive workloads. The stress-ng single-core CPU test scored 1519.49, placing it in the average category, whereas the multi-core CPU score of 3012.08 is categorized as weak. Memory bandwidth tests showed 117.2 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 36.85 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. In database and application benchmarks, the instance achieved 2.48 million operations per second in Redis SET operations and a PassMark database score of 1309.2.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the r6gd.large offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio and local NVMe storage on an energy-efficient ARM architecture, making it a resource-dense option for memory-bound workloads that do not require high compute parallelism. It is suitable for workloads such as caching layers, lightweight database operations, and memory-intensive applications that benefit from local SSD scratch space. However, its lower multi-core performance and memory bandwidth make it less suitable for heavy parallel processing, complex compilation, or large-scale LLM inference.
description: Amazon Web Services r6gd.large is a memory-optimized instance featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs powered by an arm64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it provides 16.0 GB of RAM, establishing an 8.0 GB per core memory density. The instance includes a bundled 118 GB NVMe SSD local storage volume and a baseline network speed of 1 Gbps. Performance benchmarks show average single-core capabilities, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1519.49, but weaker multi-core and memory bandwidth results, such as a multi-core score of 3012.08 and uncached read bandwidth of 36.85 GB/sec. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy, low-compute tasks. It is best suited for caching, lightweight database operations, and memory-intensive applications requiring local SSD scratch space.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6gd.large is an arm64 memory-optimized instance with 2 Graviton vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and a 118 GB NVMe SSD, designed for lightweight database and caching workloads.

Equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM, providing a high 8.0 GB per core memory density.
